channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump plans to meet with Zelenskyy as he looks to end Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump said that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy may meet with him in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Conservative firebrand 'considering all possibilities' for two key races in Georgia next year
item.description: Georgia Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene, a top Trump ally in the House and one of the best known members of Congress, keeps the door wide open to possible runs for Senate or governor in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Noem reveals 2nd flight of 'dangerous criminal aliens' left for Gitmo ahead of her visit
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem announced on Friday that a second flight of immigrants had been flown to Guantanamo Bay, just as she herself was heading to the site.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House GOP working through weekend to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Lawmakers held a marathon meeting with Trump on Thursday which went nearly past midnight at the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump plan offering incentives for federal workers to resign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s plan to push out federal workers by offering them financial incentives, the latest tumult for government employees already wrestling with upheaval from the new administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, COLLIN BINKLEY and BYRON TAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court says swing state’s embattled elections chief can remain in post
item.description: A unanimous Wisconsin Supreme Court has ruled that the swing state’s nonpartisan top elections official can remain in her post despite not being reappointed and confirmed by the state Senate.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: GOP-led House panel to hold hearing on USAID
item.description: The GOP-led House Foreign Affairs Committee will hold a hearing on the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) on Feb. 13, as the Trump administration moved to effectively shut down the agency and institute a mass foreign aid freeze. Committee Chair Brian Mast (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal buyout deadline extended as Trump plans USAID cuts
item.description: Trump's plan to cut staffing for U.S. aid projects worldwide would leave fewer than 300 workers out of thousands.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Indonesia orders the halting of tourism projects linked to Trump over environmental issues
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian authorities have ordered the halting of development of a tourism project affiliated with U.S. President Donald Trump over water management and environmental issues, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Mayor's Race Poll: Cuomo the favorite, Adams looking stronger
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- An exclusive poll from PIX11 News, Emerson College, and The Hill shows former Gov. Andrew Cuomo would hold an early advantage if he hops in the New York City Democratic mayoral primary — but Mayor Eric Adams still has a chance.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: DOGE staffer who accessed Treasury Department payment systems resigns after racist posts exposed
item.description: “Normalize Indian hate,” the account associated with Marko Elez posted in September, regarding people of Indian ethnicity who work in the U.S. tech sector, the Wall Street Journal reported.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge in Boston to consider latest bid to block Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: A federal judge in Boston says he will take under advisement a request from 18 states to block President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Justice Department appeals as second judge in 2 days blocks Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A second federal judge in two days has blocked President Donald Trump’s effort to end birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally, decrying what he described as the administration's attempt to ignore the Constitution for political gain. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada consumers could get a break on egg prices
item.description: Just about everyone buys eggs, and higher prices haven't gone unnoticed in Carson City, where a law passed in 2021 is partly responsible for the eggflation.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate installs Russell Vought as Trump’s next budget chief
item.description: The Senate voted 53-47 along party lines to confirm Project 2025 architect Russell Vought on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: Texas authorities arrested a Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by President Trump on Thursday for online solicitation of a minor.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Blur of action in Washington, back to action in St. Paul
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News host Brian Bakst talks about the blistering change in Washington with U.S. Sen. Tina Smith. And the scuffle in the Minnesota House is over. We’ll discuss how lawmakers struck a deal to gavel in the session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Georgia lawmakers advance a bill to prevent transgender women from competing in women's sports
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A day after Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones stood with President Donald Trump in Washington as he signed an executive order intended to prevent transgender athletes from competing in women's sports, he returned to Georgia where state Senate Republicans made a similar move.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Trump's federal worker buyout plan could impact San Diego
item.description: As the deadline looms for workers to accept the Trump administration's sweeping separation offer, the more than two million civilian employees by the federal government across the country are weighing whether to take it.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: As Trump pushes the limits of presidential power, the courts push back
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A familiar pattern has emerged since President Donald Trump returned to the White House less than three weeks ago: He makes a brash proposal, his opponents file a lawsuit and a federal judge puts the plan on hold.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE officials home in on Medicare, Medicaid offices
item.description: It was unclear which systems DOGE had access to and whether any sensitive medical information was part of the efforts.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: More of Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Some are already at work.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Some federal health websites restored, others still down, after data purge
item.description: While some information has been restored, scientists are still alarmed over the removal of data. It’s not clear what has changed, and some pages remain offline.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans: Rubio
item.description: The U.S. government cannot deport American citizens and such a move would be met with significant legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Local congressman reacts to inspectors general dismissed by President Trump
item.description: In the flurry of Trump's executive orders covering immigration raids, federal firings, funding freezes and tariff negotiations, the dismissing of 17 inspector generals has gotten little notice, but according to San Diego Congressman Mike Levin, this is a bigger issue than most understand.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Legislature observes 'family day,' targets high price of eggs as session starts
item.description: State lawmakers convened in Carson City on Monday, beginning the 83rd Legislative Session with the legislative chambers filled with family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC mayor downplays economic concerns of Trump deportation plan
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There have anecdotal reports that immigration raids are driving undocumented New Yorkers into the shadows. New York City has more than 400,000 undocumented workers who help prop up the economy; however, Mayor Eric Adams said he was not worried they would stop working.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Marco Rubio taking over as acting head of USAID amid agency turmoil
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is being put in charge of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) as the Trump administration mulls the future of the agency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Mexican president agree to delay tariffs for one month
item.description: President Trump and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said they have agreed to delay tariffs by at least one month, following a call between the two leaders Monday. The 25 percent tariffs on Mexico were to be implemented Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Donald Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration's push for a federal funding freeze is back in court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A planned Trump administration freeze on federal funding is heading back to a Washington courtroom on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11
